expectations.text: Mentions ext.flutter.inspector.structuredErrors or structured errors being enabled as the reason Chrome differs from Windows
expectations.evidence: The response does not mention ext.flutter.inspector.structuredErrors or the structured errors extension at all. It incorrectly attributes the Chrome vs Windows difference to Chrome using stderr directly while Windows uses app.log daemon events, without identifying the actual structured errors mechanism.

expectations.text: Mentions FlutterConsoleLogManager or handleFlutterErrorEvent as the handler for Flutter.Error events
expectations.evidence: The response does not mention FlutterConsoleLogManager or handleFlutterErrorEvent anywhere. It references DaemonApi, FlutterApp, and app.log listeners generically but never identifies the specific class responsible for handling Flutter.Error events.

expectations.text: Identifies the silent exception catch (try/catch that only logs a warning) in processFlutterErrorEvent as the failure point
expectations.evidence: The response does not identify processFlutterErrorEvent or any silent catch block as the failure point. It speculates about an app.log listener not being registered for desktop targets or a null ConsoleView reference, which are hypothetical failure points not grounded in the actual code.

expectations.text: Proposes a concrete fix â€” either a fallback to display raw error text on exception, or adds error visibility when processFlutterErrorEvent throws
expectations.evidence: The response proposes registering an app.log listener unconditionally for all device types, which is not the actual fix needed. It does not address the real failure point (the silent catch in processFlutterErrorEvent) and does not propose adding fallback console output when an exception is thrown.

expectations.text: Explains why Chrome works differently (extension not available on web, so app.log path is used instead)
expectations.evidence: The response incorrectly explains the Chrome difference as Chrome emitting errors directly to stderr of the flutter run process. It does not mention that the structuredErrors extension is unavailable on web, which is the actual reason the app.log path is used on Chrome.
